Reposystem

RepoSystem — Decentralized sovereign repository governance and verification platform

Explore Codebase Technical Specs
⚡

Type-Safe Pipelines

Built on robust functional principles and verifiable compilation contracts to guarantee runtime safety and absolute execution reliability.

🔒

Sovereign Autonomy

Engineered for decentralized infrastructure, incorporating modern zero-trust capability gates and cryptographic attestation headers.

🚀

High-Fidelity Speed

Optimized for rapid processing and lightweight compilation, ensuring minimal overhead and ultra-fast edge propagation.

Architectural Foundation

:toc: preamble

:toclevels: 3

**Railway yard for your repository ecosystem** — visual wiring layer for multi-repo component management with aspect tagging and scenario comparison.

Quick Start

[source,bash]


cargo run -- scan $REPOS_DIR

cargo run -- export --format json > web/export.json

just web-serve

Open `http://localhost:801` in a browser, then load `./export.json`.

Estate Organization

Reposystem is the *coordination layer* for the ~300-repo estate: flat clones

in `$REPOS_DIR` are the single source of truth, and `repos-monorepo` is a

generated artifact. The rule, the cross-repo `thread` runner, the generated

`repos.toml` manifest, and the migration checklist are documented in

link:ESTATE-ORGANIZATION.adoc[ESTATE-ORGANIZATION.adoc].

[source,bash]


just repos-manifest                  # regenerate repos.toml from .gitmodules

just thread status  --group X  # cross-repo thread state

just sync-aggregator --push          # regenerate repos-monorepo (Pages/mirror)

Web UI (Prototype)

The `web/` folder contains a no-build static UI for the graph:

• interactive layouts (force/grid/radial/group)

• annotations (text/box/arrow) + export

Built with casket-ssg · High-Assurance Sovereign Infrastructure
